ops.body: Fleet-wide layout regression found and fixed post-rollout: fleet-theme.css's content-measure rule (max-width: 50rem = 850px) overflowed Quarto's ~749px article grid track — grid items aren't clipped to their area — spilling 56px under the transparent right "On this page" TOC on every non-homepage page, and floating .gd-copy-page into the TOC header (user screenshot on captain-hook; reproduced pixel-identical on cc-review). Fix: max-width: min(50rem, 100%) — cc-skills 36ef1152. Verified by rect assertion on a local build and on the live deployed page (content.right 1374.5 vs TOC.left 1420, was 1476/1420); captain-hook re-rendered via single-repo Documentation dispatch (run 29821421624, green); remaining sites heal on daily guides crons. Local-verification gotcha for the record: uv's built-wheel cache for `--with <local-dir>` ignores asset-only edits even with --reinstall-package — use --with-editable when verifying gd-build changes locally.
